title: Runebound Retrospective - Episode 5 - The Mists of Zanaga
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2qron8l5-WA

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns to the treacherous jungles of Zanaga in this deep dive into Runebound's final major expansion for second edition. The Mists of Zanaga transforms the hex crawl experience with a ritual system that fundamentally changes how you approach the game—completing specific ritual challenges gradually awakens one of nine Primal Gods who become your endgame boss. With six new heroes, a host of atmospheric challenge cards, and a thematic emphasis on the curse spreading across the land, this expansion delivers some of the most engaging scenario design in the entire Runebound catalog.

The expansion shines through its flavor text and world-building, bringing the mysterious makim and corrupted lizardman empire to vivid life. The ritual tokens add compelling decision-making to every encounter, and the roaming monster track creates genuine tension even between challenge spaces. Whether you're a solo adventurer or playing competitively, the combination of new mechanics and polished theming makes this one of Daniel's most-played expansions.

----

If you've been playing through the Runebound retrospective series, what draws you most to the expansions—the new mechanics, the fresh encounters, or the opportunity to tell different stories in Taranoth?
